q: Using the given figure, the ratio of \(\mathrm{R}_f\) values of sample \(\mathrm{A}\) and sample \(\mathrm{C}\) is \(x \times 10^{-2}\). Value of \(x\) is ______
question: Using the given figure, the ratio of \(\mathrm{R}_f\) values of sample \(\mathrm{A}\) and sample \(\mathrm{C}\) is \(x \times 10^{-2}\). Value of \(x\) is ______
solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{f}} \text { of } \mathrm{A}=\frac{5}{12.5} \quad \mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{f}} \text { of } \mathrm{C}=\frac{10}{12.5} \\ & \text { Ratio }=\frac{\mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{A})}}{\mathrm{R}_{\mathrm{f}(\mathrm{C})}}=\frac{1}{2}=0.5 \text { or } 50 \times 10^{-2}\end{aligned}$

q: IUPAC name of following compound is
question: IUPAC name of following compound is
solution:
• Identify the longest carbon chain containing the functional group: The longest carbon chain is four carbons long, indicating the prefix "butan-".
  • Number the carbons in the chain: Begin numbering from the end nearest to the functional group. 
  • Identify and name substituent groups: The amino group (–NH2) is present as a substituent on carbon number 3. It is named as "amino".
  • Identify the functional group: The functional group present is the nitrile group (–CN).

Combining all parts, the IUPAC name "3-Aminobutanenitrile" is derived.

Hence, the answer is option C.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Match List I with List II

  LIST I (Technique)   LIST II (Application)
A. Distillation I. Separation of glycerol from spent-lye
B. Fractional distillation II. Aniline - Water mixture
C. Steam distillation III. Separation of crude oil fractions
D. Distillation under reduced pressure IV. Chloroform-Aniline

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
Match List I with List II

  LIST I (Technique)   LIST II (Application)
A. Distillation I. Separation of glycerol from spent-lye
B. Fractional distillation II. Aniline - Water mixture
C. Steam distillation III. Separation of crude oil fractions
D. Distillation under reduced pressure IV. Chloroform-Aniline

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
A mixture of chloroform and aniline can be separated by distillation. The boiling point of chloroform is 60°C, and that of aniline is 189°C. Therefore, distillation can be used to separate a mixture of chloroform and aniline.

Fractional distillation is the process by which oil refineries separate crude oil into different, more useful hydrocarbon products based on their relative molecular weights in a distillation tower.

Steam Distillation is a separation process for temperature-sensitive substances. It is a particular type of distillation. Another way around is a method for separating miscible liquid bases depending upon their volatilities. Aniline - Water mixture can be separated by this technique.

For separating glycerol from spent-lye is distillation under reduced pressure because glycerol has a high boiling point and since in this method the external pressure is lowered, it boils at a lower temperature.

Hence option B is the answer.

subject: Chemistry
q: Number of geometrical isomers possible for the given structure is/are _________.
question: Number of geometrical isomers possible for the given structure is/are _________.
solution:
The isomerism that arises when atoms or groups having different spatial arrangements are restricted to rotate around a bond or bonds in a molecule is called geometric isomerism. This restricted rotation mainly occurs in a carbon-carbon double bond.

The conditions to form geometrical isomers are-

  1. restricted rotation (often involving a carbon-carbon double bond);
  2. Presence of two different groups on the left-hand end of the double bond and two different groups on the right-hand end. It is not necessary to have similar kinds of groups or atoms at both ends.

In the given compound, the central double bond is not give geometrical isomerism as it contains the same group on carbon, where the terminal double bonds (two double bonds) are exhibiting geometrical isomerism. Hence, the total number of isomers is 22=4 isomers.

Hence, the given compound shows four geometrical isomers.

subject: Chemistry

question: Among the following compounds, geometrical isomerism is exhibited by :
solution:
Geometrical isomerism arises due to
(1) The presence of a restricted rotation (double bond or a ring structure).
(2) Two different groups should be attached to any two carbon atoms of restricted rotation.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Ionic reactions with organic compounds proceed through:

(A) Homolytic bond cleavage  (B) Heterolytic bond cleavage  (C) Free radical formation

(D) Primary free radical  (E) Secondary free radical

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
Ionic reactions with organic compounds proceed through:

(A) Homolytic bond cleavage  (B) Heterolytic bond cleavage  (C) Free radical formation

(D) Primary free radical  (E) Secondary free radical

Choose the correct answer from the options given below:
solution:
Heterolytic fission, also known as heterolysis, is a type of bond fission in which a covalent bond between two chemical species is broken in an unequal manner, resulting in the bond pair of electrons being retained by one of the chemical species (while the other species does not retain any of the electrons from the bond pair). When a neutrally charged molecule undergoes heterolytic fission, one of the products will have a positive charge whereas the other product will have a negative charge.
Polar reactions occur when two bonded atoms come apart, one taking more of the shared electrons than the other. They involve heterolytic cleavage. The result is two charged species—one cation and one anion.
Hence, organic reactions which proceed through heterolytic bond cleavage are called polar reactions.

In homolytic reactions, free radicals are formed and these reaction takes place in gaseous phase.

q:
During estimation of nitrogen by Dumas' method of compound $\mathrm{X}(0.42 \mathrm{~g})$ :

_____ mL of $\mathrm{N}_2$ gas will be liberated at STP. (nearest integer)
(Given molar mass in $\mathrm{g} \mathrm{mol}^{-1}: \mathrm{C}: 12, \mathrm{H}: 1$, $\mathrm{N}: 14)$
question:
During estimation of nitrogen by Dumas' method of compound $\mathrm{X}(0.42 \mathrm{~g})$ :

_____ mL of $\mathrm{N}_2$ gas will be liberated at STP. (nearest integer)
(Given molar mass in $\mathrm{g} \mathrm{mol}^{-1}: \mathrm{C}: 12, \mathrm{H}: 1$, $\mathrm{N}: 14)$
solution:
M.wt. of given compound $=86$

Applying POAC on ' N '
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{X}} \times 2=\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{N}_2} \times 2 \\
& \frac{0.42}{86}=\mathrm{n}_{\mathrm{N}_2} \\
& \Rightarrow(\text { Volume })_{\mathrm{N}_2} \text { at } \mathrm{STP}=\frac{0.42}{86} \times 22.4 \mathrm{~L} \\
& =0.1108 \mathrm{~L}=110.8 \mathrm{~m} \ell
\end{aligned}$

solution:
Since in aromatic amines the lone pair undergoes delocalisation, aliphatic amines are more basic than aromatic amines.
Ph-NH2<R-NH2
In an aliphatic amine, due to the +I effect of -CH3 the order will be 3°>2°>1°.
In an aqueous solution, H-bonding takes place. So, the order will be 1°>2°>3°.

However, the resultant basic order is due to a mixture of both the effects(+I and hydrogen bond). So, the order is 2°>3°>1°. 

So, the final basic order is

q: On the treatment of the following compound with a strong acid, the most susceptible site for bond cleavage is:
question: On the treatment of the following compound with a strong acid, the most susceptible site for bond cleavage is:
solution: The lone pair of electrons on $\mathrm{O} 2$ is involved in resonance with $\mathrm{C}=\mathrm{C}$. Hence $\mathrm{O} 2$ will not be protonated. The lone pair of electrons on O5 is not involved in resonance with $\mathrm{C}=\mathrm{C}$. Hence, O5 will be protonated. Chloride ion will then attack least substituted $\mathrm{C}$ atom (C6)

subject: Chemistry
q:
0.25 g of an organic compound " A " containing carbon, hydrogen and oxygen was analysed using the combustion method. There was an increase in mass of $\mathrm{CaCl}_{2}$ tube and potash tube at the end of the experiment. The amount was found to be 0.15 g and 0.1837 g, respectively. The percentage of oxygen in compound A is %. (Nearest integer)
(Given: molar mass in $\mathrm{g} \mathrm{mol}^{-1} \mathrm{H}: 1, \mathrm{C}: 12, \mathrm{O}: 16$)
question:
0.25 g of an organic compound " A " containing carbon, hydrogen and oxygen was analysed using the combustion method. There was an increase in mass of $\mathrm{CaCl}_{2}$ tube and potash tube at the end of the experiment. The amount was found to be 0.15 g and 0.1837 g, respectively. The percentage of oxygen in compound A is %. (Nearest integer)
(Given: molar mass in $\mathrm{g} \mathrm{mol}^{-1} \mathrm{H}: 1, \mathrm{C}: 12, \mathrm{O}: 16$)
solution:
In combustion analysis: CaCl₂ tube absorbs H₂O and potash tube absorbs CO₂.
Mass increase of CaCl₂ = 0.15 g gives moles of H₂O = 0.15/18 = 0.00833 mol, so moles of H = 0.01667 mol and mass of H = 0.01667 g.
Mass increase of potash = 0.1837 g gives moles of CO₂ = 0.1837/44 = 0.00417 mol, so moles of C = 0.00417 mol and mass of C = 0.05 g.
Mass of O = 0.25 - 0.05 - 0.01667 = 0.18333 g.
Percentage of O = (0.18333/0.25) × 100 = 73.33% ≈ 73% (nearest integer).

(b) Steam distillation separates the mixture of $o$-nitrophenol and $p$-nitrophenol. The $o$-nitrophenol is steam volatile (due to intramolecular hydrogen bonding), and the para isomer is not volatile.
(c) Fractional distillation separates crude naphtha. Naphtha is a flammable liquid hydrocarbon mixture.
(d) Distillation under reduced pressure separates mixture of glycerol and sugars. Vacuum distillation lowers the boiling point and prevents decomposition.

q: Which of the following has highly acidic hydrogen?
question: Which of the following has highly acidic hydrogen?
solution:
Acidic hydrogen is a hydrogen atom in an acid that dissociates to generate a positive ion.

After removal of H+ion, the negative charge is formed. The more stable the negative charge, the more will be the acidic strength.

Due to cross conjugation of negative charge, it is
most stabilised anion.

Hence, the answer is option D.

subject: Chemistry
q:
Given below are two statements:
Statement I: In $O_2 N$-$C_6 H_4$-$CH^{\oplus}$-$C_6 H_4$-$OCH_3$, the carbocation is stabilised by $+R$ effect of $-OCH_3$ group.
Statement II: In $O_2 N$-$C_6 H_4$-$CH^{\ominus}$-$C_6 H_4$-$OCH_3$, the carbanion is stabilised by $-R$ effect of $-NO_2$ group.
In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:
question:
Given below are two statements:
Statement I: In $O_2 N$-$C_6 H_4$-$CH^{\oplus}$-$C_6 H_4$-$OCH_3$, the carbocation is stabilised by $+R$ effect of $-OCH_3$ group.
Statement II: In $O_2 N$-$C_6 H_4$-$CH^{\ominus}$-$C_6 H_4$-$OCH_3$, the carbanion is stabilised by $-R$ effect of $-NO_2$ group.
In the light of the above statements, choose the correct answer from the options given below:
options: Both Statement I and Statement II are true
Both Statement I and Statement II are false
Statement I is true but Statement II is false
Statement I is false but Statement II is true
solution:
Statement I is true because the $-OCH_3$ group is an electron-donating group. It donates electron density through resonance ($+R$ effect), which delocalizes the positive charge of the carbocation and stabilizes it.

Statement II is true because the $-NO_2$ group is a strong electron-withdrawing group. It withdraws electron density through resonance ($-R$ effect), which delocalizes the negative charge of the carbanion and stabilizes it.

Therefore, both Statement I and Statement II are true.

Answer: Both Statement I and Statement II are true

subject: Chemistry
q: A species having carbon with sextet of electrons and can act as electrophile is called
question: A species having carbon with sextet of electrons and can act as electrophile is called
solution:
A carbocation is a molecule in which a carbon atom has a positive charge and three bonds. We can say that, basically, they are carbon cations. Formerly, it was known as carbonium ion. Carbocation today is defined as any even-electron cation that possesses a significant positive charge on the carbon atom.

The carbon cations are very reactive and unstable due to an incomplete octet. In simple words, carbocations do not have eight electrons; therefore, they do not satisfy the octet rule.

In carbocation, the hybridisation of carbon will be sp2 and its shape is trigonal planar. There is also a vacant p- orbital which indicates its electron-deficient nature. The carbon has 6 electrons in its valence shell. Due to this, it is an electron-deficient species, also known as an electrophile.

Six electron species
